factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-Israel Escalation)
factors.summary: Iran fired multiple missile barrages at Israel after Israeli strikes on Beirut; fragile ceasefire jeopardized. Traditionally bullish for gold as safe-haven bid, though initial reaction shows gold declining per Bloomberg 'Gold Holds Decline as Iran Attacks Threaten Mideast Ceasefire.'

factors.name: US Dollar Index & Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: AUD/USD weakened to 0.7024 and broader risk-off tone favoring USD strength per FXStreet; stronger USD typically inversely correlated with gold. USD appears to be benefiting from de-risking rather than gold being chosen as safe-haven.

factors.name: US Jobs Data & Real Yields
factors.summary: Strong US jobs report (mentioned multiple times) fuels Fed rate-hike expectations; bond traders wagering on bigger CPI surge this week per Bloomberg 'Bond Traders Bet on a CPI Surge.' Higher real yields (if inflation data confirms) are highly negative for gold.

factors.name: Tech Selloff & Equity Volatility
factors.summary: Massive tech rout on Friday followed by mixed stock futures Sunday; risk-off environment could support gold, but competing USD strength and higher rate expectations are offsetting.

factors.name: Oil Prices Surge
factors.summary: Oil jumped >$2 on Middle East escalation (Bloomberg, FXStreet); higher energy costs feed inflation narrative, which paradoxically can hurt gold if it raises real yields via Fed tightening expectations.

factors.name: Japan Q1 GDP Upside (1.8% vs 1.3% expected)
factors.summary: Strong Japanese GDP supports yen and signals possible BoJ tightening risk; USD/JPY above 160 with intervention risk. Firmer yen reduces yen-carry trades' profitability but does not directly impact XAUUSD directionally without DXY move.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS yield data in news window, but strong jobs + expected CPI print this week imply real yields likely to rise or remain elevated; single most important negative for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Stance
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision in this window, but strong employment data and bond traders expecting CPI surge suggest market is pricing in delayed rate cuts or even hike risk; hawkish backdrop.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No specific data on GLD/IAU holdings or Indian/Chinese physical demand in this 24h window.

factors.name: Trump's Iran Deal Push
factors.summary: Trump urging restraint and signaling negotiation willingness ('Iran close to a deal'); de-escalation talk reduces geopolitical premium risk but also removes acute safe-haven bid.

screening.narrative: Gold faced a curious paradox over the weekend: Iran's direct missile attack on Israel—a classic safe-haven catalyst—instead saw gold decline while USD strengthened, signaling risk-off into dollar and duration rather than precious metals. The underlying driver is a hawkish rate backdrop: strong US jobs data and consensus for a significant CPI surprise this week (per Bloomberg bond traders) are raising real-yield expectations, which is the single largest headwind for gold. Geopolitical tension alone cannot overcome the macro gravity of higher real yields and Fed tightening. Trump's push for a negotiated Iran deal also reduces the escalation premium. The move appears to be in Phase 2 (risk-off into USD, down ~0.5–1% from Friday highs), and gold is being left behind.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/Core CPI print (this week, likely Wed/Thu) — major inflation surprise could reverse entire narrative
FOMC meeting or Powell speech — any dovish signal would flip gold sharply bullish
Iran-Israel further escalation or ceasefire announcement — geopolitical relief could reduce safe-haven bid further
Apple WWDC 2026 (Monday) — indirect: tech sentiment marker
UK GDP, ECB/BoC meetings later in week — secondary but could shift global rate expectations
screening.risk_warning: Primary invalidation: if US CPI comes in cooler-than-expected (sub-consensus), real yields fall, and gold would reverse higher sharply. Secondary: if Iran-Israel tensions escalate into direct war (beyond missile exchange), safe-haven bid could override rate logic and spike gold. Tertiary: if Fed signals dovish pivot in coming days, gold rallies.
screening.entry_guidance: Gold in Phase 2 decline; avoid chasing shorts. Wait for pullback to 2310–2320 support or watch for CPI surprise data point before taking directional conviction. If CPI prints hot (as consensus expects), look for short entry on failed bounce to 2360 resistance.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY) strength
factors.summary: DXY holding steady near 100.10 amid Middle East tensions and rising Fed hike bets. Strong USD typically inverse to gold; multiple sources confirm USD bid.

factors.name: Fed rate-hike expectations (hawkish surprise)
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs shifted dovish Dec 2025 rate-cut call to June 2027 on strong Friday jobs data; market now pricing December 2025 hike + potential September hike. This is a material hawkish re-pricing that crushes real yields.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: With nominal Treasury yields rising on Fed hike bets and inflation expectations moderating (no recent CPI surprise noted), real yields are grinding higher, directly pressuring gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran-Israel escalation)
factors.summary: Iran and Israel exchanged missile strikes over the weekend; Strait of Hormuz transit concerns, oil jumped >3% to $90+. Normally safe-haven bullish for gold, but USD/oil correlation is dominating; oil strength fuels inflation fears, strengthening Fed hike bets (which crushes gold via real yields).

factors.name: Risk sentiment (tech selloff, equity weakness)
factors.summary: Asian markets collapsed Monday (Kospi circuit breaker, Nikkei -3.5%, Korean stocks down 8%+ at open) after Friday's Nasdaq -4.2% rout; AI bubble unwinding. Typically safe-haven bullish for gold, but USD strength is overpowering the flight-to-safety bid.

factors.name: Gold price action and technicals
factors.summary: Gold stuck in downtrend at $4,339 (per FXStreet 05:06), touches lowest level since March 23, vulnerable below March lows. Silver also in steep downtrend (RSI 24.99). Price action confirms seller pressure.

factors.name: Inflation surprise status
factors.summary: No new CPI/PCE print in news window; last major data was Friday's jobs report (strong, cited by GS for hawkish shift). No hot inflation surprise, but expectations have re-anchored higher on Fed tightening signal.

factors.name: ECB rate decision (imminent)
factors.summary: ECB meets this week; economists warn of 2011 mistake if they hike into slowdown. If ECB cuts or stalls, EUR weakness could support DXY, further pressuring gold. Hawkish surprise would also lift rates globally.

factors.name: BoE (Bank of England) policy
factors.summary: Taylor sees rates on hold barring worst-case scenario; no major shift implied. BoE seen as steady, not a driver today.

factors.name: Emerging market stress (Indonesia rupiah, Indian rupee)
factors.summary: USD/IDR hit all-time high 18,210; USD/INR near 95.30 on hawkish Fed bets; Indonesia FX reserves declining. EM weakness supports USD bid (safe-haven flow), but structural, not geopolitical-driven.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a bear trap as the confluence of hawkish Fed repricing (Goldman Sachs kill-shot on rate-cut expectations, market pricing December 2025 hike) and aggressive US Dollar strength (DXY 100.10, fed by rising real yields) overwhelms the typical safe-haven bid from Iran-Israel escalation and equity selloff. Gold's downtrend since March is intact; price at $4,339 represents fresh 11-week lows. The critical inversion is that geopolitical risk (oil +3%, Strait of Hormuz threats) is amplifying inflation-hike-scenario fears, which strengthens the Fed hawkish narrative and crushes real yields—the single biggest gold headwind. This is a PHASE_2 move already underway (gold down ~$40 from Friday close, approaching March lows), so conviction is moderate despite factor alignment.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision (this week, likely Wed 12 June) — if hawkish hike or less dovish than expected, further EUR weakness + global rate support could extend USD/gold bearish move
UK inflation data (early week) — could influence BoE/BoJ carry-unwind scenarios
Next US CPI/PCE print (mid-June) — critical for real-yield repricing; any upside surprise would cement Fed hike bets and crush gold
US 2-year yield trajectory into FOMC — tracking at elevated levels; if breaks higher, real rates accelerate upward
Middle East escalation de-escalation news (ongoing) — any ceasefire agreement would remove geopolitical risk premium, further isolating gold as a real-yield play
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-Israel conflict escalates dramatically (e.g., Strait of Hormuz blockade, major supply shock) or if a major financial stress event (credit spreads widen >50bps, equity VIX spike >35) forces a flight-to-safety bid that overwhelms Fed hawkish repricing, gold could reverse sharply higher and break above $4,350. Additionally, if ECB surprises dovish (rate cut signaled) or next CPI prints cool (below expected), real yields could compress rapidly, invalidating the bearish thesis. Conversely, a break and close below $4,300 would target $4,250–$4,200.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Hawkishness
factors.summary: Strong NFP report (Friday) with higher revisions has fully priced in Fed tightening risk. Market now expects potential rate hikes this year under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, pushing real yields higher and directly pressuring gold.

factors.name: USD Strength (DXY)
factors.summary: USD Index posting strong performance across major pairs (EUR/USD at ~1.1515, GBP/USD at ~1.3338 near 3-week lows). Hawkish Fed bets and geopolitical safe-haven bid supporting dollar; inverse correlation to gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations / CPI Surprises
factors.summary: Hot NFP data (revisions upward, unemployment falling to 4.29%) signals inflation resilience, forcing bond traders to wager on largest consumer price surge in several years per Bloomberg. This supports Fed tightening, pressuring gold.

factors.name: Nominal Treasury Yields
factors.summary: Bond yields rising on inflation fears and hawkish Fed repricing. 10-year nominal yields moving higher compresses gold's relative attractiveness as a non-yielding asset.

factors.name: Geopolitical Safe-Haven (Israel-Iran)
factors.summary: Iran-Israel missile exchanges and escalation risk typically bid safe-havens (gold, USD, yen), but USD strength is dominating the narrative. Oil prices jumping to ~$92-96/bbl adds inflation concern that feeds bearish real yields story.

factors.name: Central Bank Positioning
factors.summary: BoJ tightening bets lifting JPY; ECB delivering 25bp hike Thursday. Both reduce relative gold appeal vs higher-yielding alternatives and support USD carry unwinding headwinds.

factors.name: Price Action / Technical
factors.summary: Gold at $4,324 is already near 'fresh monthly lows' per FXStreet (lowest since March 23 per 08:32 report). Extended downside move has already occurred; 24h change -0.30% confirms weakness into this session.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Tech selloff (AI stock plunge Friday, South Korea index down ~9%) suggests flight-to-safety bid should support gold, but strong dollar and Fed hawkishness are overwhelming that bid. Equities posting modest rebounds.

screening.narrative: Gold tumbled to March lows following hot Friday NFP data (strong job gains, lower unemployment) that fully repriced Fed tightening expectations under new Chair Kevin Warsh. Real yields are rising on inflation resilience fears, and the USD is strengthening broadly (EUR/USD, GBP/USD both breaking lower) as safe-haven bid flows to the dollar rather than gold. Middle East escalation (Iran-Israel strikes) is lifting oil and inflation concerns, paradoxically reinforcing hawkish Fed repricing that hurts real gold. The move has already extended significantly; gold is in PHASE_3 downtrend.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision + guidance (Thursday 2026-06-12, expected 25bp hike but forward guidance critical for EUR/gold cross)
US CPI/Core CPI (date TBA next week, critical for inflation narrative and real yield direction)
FOMC meeting under new Chair Warsh (date TBA, will test independence vs Trump pressure for cuts)
Any Trump-Iran ceasefire update (could reduce oil premium, ease inflation fears, and reverse Fed tightening bets)
Further Fed speakers / Warsh commentary on policy path
screening.risk_warning: Bias INVALIDATED if: (1) Surprise CPI miss (cool inflation print) forces Fed pivot to easing, crushing real yields higher and reinvigorating gold; (2) Israel-Iran escalates into major regional conflict (Strait of Hormuz closure risk), oil spikes sharply and forces risk-off flight-to-quality bid into gold despite hawkish Fed; (3) Tech selloff deepens and credit spreads blow out, overriding Fed narrative and driving broad safe-haven demand including gold; (4) ECB guidance turns dovish Thursday, reversing USD strength.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Fed funds futures now pricing in tightening (not easing per Investinglive 12:09), and Schwab analyst Martin suggests 'case for Fed to hike right now.' Higher real yields are headwinds for gold. No fresh 10y TIPS print in the news window, but repricing is clearly bearish.

factors.name: Fed Policy Rhetoric
factors.summary: Hawkish repricing dominates: stronger-than-expected US payrolls have 'sharply reduced expectations for rate cuts in 2026' (UOB, 13:31); Schwab notes bar to action 'getting lower' for a hike. This is a fresh shift from earlier dovish bets.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD mostly lower to start the week (Investinglive 12:09), after Friday's sharp gains. However, Fed hawkish repricing and risk-off flows are supporting DXY per ING (12:45). Mixed intraday action; stronger USD near-term is a headwind for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Middle East)
factors.summary: Iran and Israel exchanged missile strikes Sunday night (first since April ceasefire), with Brent/WTI surging 4-5% (BNY 12:02). Though Iran announced 'end of military operations' (Investinglive 11:56) and ceasefire is being pursued by Trump, volatility remains elevated—a traditional safe-haven bid for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Oil touched ~$98/bbl with 4-5% surge on Iran-Israel tensions, though erased most gains after ceasefire signals per MarketWatch (12:39). Oil remains volatile and above $95, supporting inflation expectations and safe-haven demand.

factors.name: Equity Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: US stocks rebounded from worst rout this year, but technical picture remains mixed (Investinglive 14:33). NASDAQ holds 'short-term bearish bias' despite rebound; S&P more neutral. Risk-off regime supports gold, but equity rebound suggests risk appetite is stabilizing.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows / Physical Demand
factors.summary: No specific GLD/IAU flow data in news window. Seasonality not relevant (not Q4 India wedding season or Chinese New Year period).

factors.name: Other Central Banks (ECB, BoE, BoJ)
factors.summary: ECB expected to deliver one-off 25bp 'insurance' hike to 2.25% per OCBC (13:53); BoE expected to continue tightening per BBH (12:12). Higher global yields are headwinds. BoC expected to hold at 2.25% (TD Securities 14:07)—no immediate policy support for gold.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent & Upcoming)
factors.summary: US Employment Trends Index fell to 107.01 in May (Investinglive 14:04), signaling moderation in labor market. TD Securities expects May CPI to show 'moderating but still-elevated core inflation' at 0.23% m/m, 2.8% y/y (12:11)—modest surprise upside risk, but disinflation narrative intact.

factors.name: Price Action & Recent Move
factors.summary: 4h change -0.22%, 24h change +0.22%—essentially flat to slightly positive over the day. Gold hit 'two-and-a-half-month low' before recovering per FXStreet (12:18). Move is choppy; no strong directional conviction yet.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught between two opposing forces: hawkish Fed repricing (triggered by strong US payrolls on Friday and market expectations for potential rate hikes, not cuts) is bearish for real yields and XAU/USD, while Middle East geopolitical escalation (Iran-Israel strikes) provides a safe-haven bid. The bounce from a 2.5-month low to current levels ($4,346) suggests some bid is being triggered by tension premium and technicals, but the Fed hawkish tone is heavier. Equity rebound also eases risk-off demand. Intraday price action remains flat—suggesting indecision and no sustained directional momentum yet.
catalysts_ahead: US May CPI / Core CPI release (likely early June week, exact date not specified in news but flagged as imminent per TD Securities)
US May PPI release (flagged as key data in coming days)
FOMC decision / Powell remarks (expected mid-June but not yet scheduled in this window)
Further Middle East escalation/ceasefire confirmation (Netanyahu decision on escalation this evening per Investinglive 13:57)
Nvidia earnings / tech sector strength (ongoing rebound could further dampen safe-haven demand)
G7 / ECB policy decisions (ECB hike expected soon, per OCBC)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed hawkish repricing accelerates (via hot CPI print or more explicit Fed hike guidance), real yields spike and gold could fall through $4,330 support toward $4,250–$4,300. Conversely, if Middle East tensions re-escalate sharply or ceasefire collapses, safe-haven bid could push gold above $4,380. Equity rally continuing would also weigh on gold by easing risk-off demand. US economic data softening would be most bullish (lower real yields, dovish Fed repricing).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (choppy rebound from lows; no sustained momentum). Wait for either a fresh break above $4,370 resistance on geopolitical escalation, or a confirmed pullback to $4,320–$4,330 support with a bullish reversal pattern before committing. Bias is genuinely neutral until real yields or DXY direction clarifies further post-CPI.
